name: ssh
description: SSH remote access patterns and utilities. Connect to servers, manage keys, tunnels, and transfers.
capabilities: Connect to remote servers using SSH with various authentication methods
Transfer files securely using SCP and rsync protocols
Set up SSH tunnels for local and remote port forwarding
Generate and manage SSH keys including Ed25519 and RSA types
Configure jump hosts and bastion servers for internal access
Use SSH multiplexing for efficient connection reuse
author: Dicklesworthstone
securitySummary: Documentation skill providing SSH reference patterns. Static scanner flagged 151 potential issues, but all are false positives from markdown code examples. The skill contains only documentation with SSH command examples in code blocks. No executable code, network requests, or credential access. The skill promotes security best practices including Ed25519 keys and key-based authentication.
valueStatement: Managing remote servers securely requires reliable SSH patterns. This skill provides documentation for SSH connections, key management, file transfers, and tunneling for Claude Code.
limitations: This skill provides documentation patterns only, not executable code
Does not store or manage credentials - users provide their own
Does not automate key rotation or certificate management
Does not provide interactive terminal session handling

useCases.title: Connect to cloud servers securely
useCases.description: Establish SSH connections to cloud servers with proper key authentication and port configuration.

useCases.title: Transfer deployment files
useCases.description: Copy application artifacts and configuration files to remote servers using SCP or rsync.

useCases.title: Access internal services through tunnels
useCases.description: Create SSH tunnels to access databases and services behind firewalls or in private networks.

promptTemplates.title: Basic SSH connection
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to connect to a remote server using SSH with a specific port and identity file.
promptTemplates.scenario: Connecting to a server with non-standard port and key-based auth

promptTemplates.prompt: What are the commands to copy files to and from a remote server using SCP and rsync?

promptTemplates.title: Port forwarding setup
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I set up an SSH tunnel to access a database on a remote server through a jump host?

promptTemplates.prompt: What SSH key types are recommended, and how do I generate them and copy public keys to servers?

bestPractices: Use Ed25519 keys instead of RSA for better security and performance
Configure SSH multiplexing to reuse connections and reduce latency
Set up jump hosts for accessing servers in private networks securely
antiPatterns: Using password authentication instead of key-based auth
Connecting with verbose flags in production scripts
Hardcoding connection details instead of using SSH config files

faq.question: What SSH key type should I use?
faq.answer: Use Ed25519 keys for modern systems. They offer better security with smaller key sizes and faster generation. Use RSA (4096 bits) only for legacy systems that do not support Ed25519.

faq.question: How do I avoid entering my passphrase repeatedly?
faq.answer: Use ssh-agent to cache your decrypted keys. Run 'eval "$(ssh-agent -s)"' then 'ssh-add ~/.ssh/id_ed25519' to load your key once per session.

faq.question: What is the difference between SCP and rsync?
faq.answer: SCP is simpler for quick file copies. Rsync is better for directories and repeated syncs because it only copies changed files and offers better compression options.

faq.question: How do I access a server behind a firewall?
faq.answer: Use a jump host (bastion). Connect through it with 'ssh -J jumphost user@internal-server' or configure ProxyJump in your SSH config file.

faq.question: What is SSH multiplexing and why should I use it?
faq.answer: Multiplexing reuses a single SSH connection for multiple commands, reducing connection overhead. Configure it in ~/.ssh/config with ControlMaster, ControlPath, and ControlPersist options.

faq.question: How do I troubleshoot SSH connection issues?
faq.answer: Use verbose mode: 'ssh -v user@host' for standard output, '-vv' for more details, or '-vvv' for maximum verbosity to see exactly what is happening during connection negotiation.
